$note: Contract notes only. Updated for @adhd/agent-registry Decision 5 (head/version split) so the importer builds against the corrected component shape. Does NOT alter this plan's state.json/dag.json.

registry-component-import.how: import-pipeline writes via ComponentStore + CompositionStore against the ONE shared SQLite handle.

component-model.shape: head/version split (Decision 5): each imported .md body section becomes a registry_components head row (created once per slug) plus a registry_component_versions row at its version. UNIQUE(slug, version) + the slug→head FK are enforced, so an importer that emits a version for a missing head, or a duplicate (slug, version), now THROWS instead of silently inserting.
component-model.create-vs-version: ComponentStore.create(slug,...) inserts head + v1 atomically for a NEW slug; ComponentStore.version(slug, content) appends a new version row to an EXISTING head. Content-hash dedup (SEED_DATA §0 step 5) decides which: a new head for an unseen slug, a new version row for changed content of an existing slug, or a no-op reuse.
component-model.junction: CompositionStore.attach writes registry_agent_components with component_slug as an enforced FK → registry_components.slug and version_pin (null on import) as an enforced nullable FK → registry_component_versions.version_id. Pin an exact version with attach({ pinVersion }) or a pre-resolved version_id with attach({ versionPin }).
